entry.title: Washington Post: 13 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • House tries to avoid messy political battles over Pentagon policy bill
  • UNESCO: Nearly half of Telegram’s Holocaust content contains denial, distortion
  • Chaos in Sri Lanka after protesters seize prime minister’s office
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Turkey hosts talks on grain blockade; No final deal, U.N. chief says
  • Rishi, Kemi, Suella. The historically diverse race to be U.K. prime minister.
  • How the next U.K. prime minister will be chosen
  • At least 900,000 Ukrainians ‘forcibly deported’ to Russia, U.S. says
  • John Bolton said he planned foreign coups. The global outcry was swift.
  • Biden was to limit handshakes on this trip. He’s having trouble doing it.

  • The U.S. dollar and the euro are equal for the first time in two decades

  • The man who has Putin’s ear — and may want his job
  • Putin to visit Tehran; Russia, Ukraine to discuss Black Sea ‘grain corridor’

  • Democratic cities in Republican states seek ways around abortion bans
  • Eagles lyrics notes were stolen years ago. Now three men face charges.
  • In CNN interview, John Bolton says he has planned foreign coups
  • NYC mayor defends nuclear-attack PSA: ‘Better safe than sorry’
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says inflation ‘unacceptably high’ but not as bad as it looks
  • Who is Patrick Byrne, former Overstock CEO and election denier?
  • Mexico’s president meets Biden amid tension over migration, fentanyl
  • ‘Unhinged’: The White House meeting that preceded Trump’s ‘will be wild’ tweet
  • Trump hid plan for Capitol march on day he marked as ‘wild’, panel says

  • Judge freezes assets of crypto hedge fund Three Arrows Capital

  • Five reasons why you shouldn’t buy a house right now

  • The best couch co-op games to play with friends

  • After backlash, Ubisoft says it isn’t revoking access to owned games
  • Distinguished homes for sale in the D.C. region
  • Madison Manor is a place to come home to
  • Let’s catch up with our readers
  • Affordability, wealth gap persist in housing market, report finds

  • Hiking Rates Early Won’t Mean Catching a Break Later
  • Private Equity Targets a Risky Stagflation Bet

  • Why the Euro Has Tumbled to Parity Against the Dollar
  • How China Is Opening Up Further to Global Investors

  • We must purge our politics of Trump’s extremist poison
  • Booster shot confusion: get them now, or wait for better?
  • This is what it means to rape a 10-year-old
  • BA.5 should not change how Americans think about living with covid
  • Unlike Biden, most Democratic voters want strict abortion limits
  • The Abe assassination reminds us that individuals make history
  • Trump allowed Iran to go nuclear, but Biden will get the blame
  • NASA’s new images represent humanity — and government — at its best
  • A new and much-needed source of inspiration arrives in the Capitol
  • Biden’s offshore drilling plan won’t make or break his climate agenda
  • Dissatisfied with government? Well, federal workers are pretty unhappy, too.
  • Democratic governors show the fight their voters crave
  • Even as it flourishes, Israel faces a growing demographic challenge
  • Cartoon by Mike Luckovich
  • Youngkin’s biggest problem on abortion isn’t Democrats
  • Train travel for all is still a dream
  • Van Ness welcomes its lower-income neighbors
  • Either there is a right to privacy or there isn’t
  • Where the conspiracy starts
  • What’s a little autocracy for a little oil?
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world

  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 13 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 13 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-13 23:08PM.
entry.content:

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
